inicio.rol: Java · Spring Boot · Angular · 3 years of experience
inicio.hint: # type 'help' to list the available commands
inicio.bio: I like building things that solve real problems: from a bot to stop my friends wasting hours, to applications running in production in the banking sector. I'm comfortable moving from the backend to the frontend, and I care about code that's easy to understand and built to last. Usually with a new side project on the go.
help: sudo hire adrian   start the hiring process
hire: Press Y to continue / N to cancel

proyectos.hint: # Click on any project to see more details

proyectos.descripcion: Full-stack platform that analyzes the World of Warcraft auction house, detects market opportunities and notifies via Discord in real time
caracteristicas: Multi-module Maven architecture (auth, persistence, analysis, batch and bot kept separate)
Spring Batch pipeline that pulls prices from the Blizzard API every hour
Market analysis: buy/sell signals, volatility and cross-realm arbitrage
Discord bot (JDA) with slash commands and per-user personalized notifications
Discord login (OAuth2) and authorization via JWT
WoW addon in Lua to export your inventory straight to the bot
proyectos.problema: My friends and I were spending hours farming items to sell on the auction house instead of enjoying the raids and dungeons, which is the fun part of the game. The alternative was buying gold with real money, but not everyone could afford it. So I built a platform that watches the market and tells us what to buy and sell to make gold efficiently, without grinding for hours.
proyectos.aprendizajes: It's my largest project: 8 months of development, ~15,000 lines and a multi-module architecture. I learned to design a data pipeline with Spring Batch, integrate OAuth2 with external APIs (Blizzard and Discord) and orchestrate several services. The next step is widening test coverage over the analysis logic and adding metrics to the batch.

proyectos.descripcion: Official live website for a national craftsmen association: admin panel, Stripe e-commerce and automated newsletter
caracteristicas: Live site running in production for a real association
Admin panel with a PHP (PDO) REST API and JWT authentication
E-commerce store with Stripe payments and webhooks
Automated newsletter with double opt-in and SMTP delivery
Craftsmen map with Leaflet + OpenStreetMap and SEO with structured data (Schema.org)
proyectos.problema: A national craftsmen association needed a professional website they could manage themselves —events, craftsmen, shop and newsletter— without calling a developer for every change. The challenge was giving them full autonomy through a complete admin panel.
proyectos.aprendizajes: My first production project with a real client: I learned that reliability and ease of use for whoever administers it matter more than any flashy tech. I built it in vanilla JS and PHP on purpose, for deployment simplicity; today I'd structure the API with a framework and add automated tests.

proyectos.descripcion: Suite of published reusable libraries (Spring Boot + Angular) to build professional web apps in a fraction of the time
caracteristicas: Modular suite: 10 backend libraries (Spring Boot) and 14 frontend (Angular) for auth, users, e-commerce, CMS, reservations and more
Published and versioned on GitHub Packages (Maven and npm)
Nx monorepo that organizes and builds the frontend libraries
Continuous publishing with GitHub Actions
Design system with a configurable 4-color theme and dark mode
proyectos.problema: Every time I started a new project I rewrote the same things: auth, users, e-commerce, CMS, emails... I decided to extract all that repeated work into versioned, published libraries, to set up the foundation of any app in hours instead of days.
proyectos.aprendizajes: Designing code meant to be consumed by other projects (and my future self) forced me to care about semantic versioning, backward compatibility and documenting each module. Setting up the publish CI/CD on GitHub Packages and an Nx monorepo was the closest thing to product work I've done.

proyectos.descripcion: Source code of this website: an Angular frontend with a Linux terminal concept and its own Spring Boot API
caracteristicas: Interactive terminal theme with commands (help, snake, sudo hire)
Built with signals and Angular's new control flow
Spring Boot REST API that serves projects, experience and education per language
Spanish/English internationalization (Transloco on the front, a ?lang= param on the API)
Light/dark theme and fully responsive design
Frontend on Vercel and backend containerized with Docker on Render
proyectos.problema: My previous portfolio was two years old and no longer reflected my level. I wanted to rebuild it with a memorable identity —an interactive Linux terminal concept— and use it to show backend too, serving the data from my own Spring Boot API.
proyectos.aprendizajes: I deepened signals and Angular's new control flow, built i18n and theme switching from scratch, and wrote the API that feeds the site with a Render deployment. The biggest lesson was judgment: balancing a flashy concept (the terminal, the game, the commands) with clear, accessible navigation.

proyectos.descripcion: Secure full-stack password manager: Angular client and Spring Boot API
caracteristicas: AES password encryption with CryptoJS
User authentication and management
Password CRUD over a REST API
MySQL database
Backend containerized with Docker
proyectos.problema: I wanted my own password manager to understand first-hand how to encrypt sensitive data on the client before storing it, and to build the full client-server cycle with authentication.
proyectos.aprendizajes: It was my first serious full-stack project with Angular and Spring Boot. Today I clearly see its weak point: the encryption key is fixed and shared. I'd rebuild it with per-user derived keys and real secret management, and add integration tests to the API.

proyectos.descripcion: Petrol station finder based on your location (final degree project)
caracteristicas: MVVM architecture
Google Maps API integration
Firebase authentication
proyectos.problema: It was my final degree project (DAM, 2024). I wanted to solve something everyday —finding the cheapest petrol station near you— and learn modern native mobile development with Kotlin and Jetpack Compose along the way.
proyectos.aprendizajes: My first complete mobile app: I learned Jetpack Compose, MVVM architecture and integrating external services (Google Maps, Firebase). Today I'd take more care with state management and cache the price API calls to reduce data usage.

tareas: Development of applications for critical banking processes with Java, Spring Boot, Spring Batch and Angular.
Microservices and full lifecycle: technical analysis, new features and continuous integration/deployment with Jenkins.
Version control with Bitbucket, agile management in Jira (Scrum/Kanban) and database maintenance with SQL Developer.
Best practices: SOLID principles, automated testing, clean code and maintainable architecture.
Development of the client's new application with HTML, CSS, JS, PHP, jQuery and .NET.
Design and maintenance of the database for the new project (SQL).
Development and improvement of the company's CRM with VB.NET.
Vocational training internship in Salesforce development.
Backend and frontend development using technologies such as Lightning Web Components and Apex.
Development of a bot for process automation.
Development and optimization of the company's software with VB.NET.
Maintenance and management of SQL databases.
Development of multi-purpose applications in Java.

formacion.competencias: Higher vocational degree in full-stack and cross-platform development: object-oriented programming with Java, data access with SQL and Hibernate, and mobile app development. Final project: Gas-Path, a native Android app built with Kotlin and Jetpack Compose.

formacion.grado: Amazon Web Services (AWS) Fundamentals and Practice
formacion.centro: Fundación Estatal para la Formación en el Empleo (FUNDAE)
formacion.competencias: Cloud Computing fundamentals on AWS: core services, security and identity (IAM), databases, management tools (Auto Scaling, CloudWatch, Elastic Load Balancing, Trusted Advisor) and Hands-on Labs practice.

formacion.centro: Universidad Nacional de Educación a Distancia (UNED)
formacion.competencias: University education in advanced programming, algorithms and data structures, and operating systems. Planning to resume it to complete the degree.
